title: scan.ts detectExistingLanguage — CJK signal returns zh-CN-hybrid (not zh-CN) + rename runtime read sites to fabric_language
description: Update the detectExistingLanguage helper to return 'zh-CN-hybrid' instead of 'zh-CN' when the CJK signal is detected over README + docs/*.md. The default value remains 'match-existing' on no signal. Also rename all function-local and module-level runtime read sites from knowledge_language to fabric_language (L191 fabricConfig.knowledge_language → fabricConfig.fabric_language, comments at L115/L186/L341/L557/L572/L656). Consider also renaming the resolveKnowledgeLanguage helper to resolveFabricLanguage.

files.target: L186-L192 config load + L557-L568 resolveKnowledgeLanguage + L586-L644 detectExistingLanguage + comments at L115/L341/L572/L656
files.change: Update detectExistingLanguage (~L586-L644): when CJK signal detected (CJK ratio over threshold across README + docs/*.md), return 'zh-CN-hybrid' instead of 'zh-CN'. The 'en' return path stays. The empty-repo path still returns the default ('match-existing' or null per current contract). Rename runtime read site at L191: `fabricConfig.knowledge_language ?? 'match-existing'` → `fabricConfig.fabric_language ?? 'match-existing'`. Rename helper function `resolveKnowledgeLanguage` → `resolveFabricLanguage` (function name + all call sites). Update all comments referencing 'knowledge_language' → 'fabric_language' (L115, L186, L341, L557, L572, L656). The exported __testing__ namespace at L1534 must update its exposed function name.

Update the CJK-heavy README test expectation from 'zh-CN' to 'zh-CN-hybrid'. The other three tests (empty repo, EN-only README, docs/ scan) keep their existing expectations. Add an assertion to the CJK test that explicitly comments why zh-CN-hybrid is the correct expectation ('CJK signal in README + docs implies mixed Chinese-narrative-with-English-technical-terms; pure zh-CN is intentional opt-in, not auto-detected.').

implementation: Read scan.ts to confirm exact line numbers for detectExistingLanguage, resolveKnowledgeLanguage, and runtime config read sites
In detectExistingLanguage: identify the branch where CJK signal returns 'zh-CN'; change the return literal to 'zh-CN-hybrid'
Rename the helper resolveKnowledgeLanguage → resolveFabricLanguage; find-replace all internal call sites and the __testing__ export at L1534
Rename the runtime config read fabricConfig.knowledge_language → fabricConfig.fabric_language (L191)
Find-replace all comment text 'knowledge_language' → 'fabric_language' at L115/L186/L341/L557/L572/L656
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test scan-builders` and verify the CJK test asserts 'zh-CN-hybrid'
Run full scan-related tests: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test scan` and verify exit 0 (depends on TASK-003 being merged for the schema to accept fabric_language)

reference.pattern: Language-detection probe: detectExistingLanguage measures CJK character ratio over README + docs/*.md and returns one of three values. Now extended to return the new zh-CN-hybrid value on CJK signal, since real-world CJK projects almost always preserve English technical terms.
reference.examples: Match the existing detectExistingLanguage return-value convention; the function is the canonical decision point for init-time language fixation.

criteria: scan.ts detectExistingLanguage returns 'zh-CN-hybrid' on CJK signal (verify via test)
scan.ts has zero 'knowledge_language' references (grep returns empty)
Helper function renamed to resolveFabricLanguage; all call sites updated; __testing__ export updated
scan-builders.test.ts CJK test expects 'zh-CN-hybrid'; other three tests pass unchanged
Full scan test suite passes (`pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test scan` exit 0)
Dogfood validation: running detectExistingLanguage against THIS project's root returns 'zh-CN-hybrid' (the project is CJK-heavy README + English Skill technical terms — the canonical hybrid case)

rationale.chosen_approach: Auto-detection defaults to the more practical zh-CN-hybrid (preserves technical terms) rather than the stricter pure zh-CN. Users who want strict monolingual zh-CN must opt in by editing fabric-config.json — this matches actual UX expectations for CJK projects.
alternatives_considered: Keep zh-CN as auto-detect default; require user to edit config for hybrid (rejected: forces every CJK user to edit config; contradicts the principle of best-default detection)
Add a second config field 'preserve_technical_terms: boolean' (rejected: doubles the config surface for a single decision; enum value is cleaner)
Auto-detect by also probing for English code-block ratio (rejected: more complex; the four enum values already capture the meaningful axes)
decision_factors: Real-world CJK projects almost always preserve English technical terms
Single enum-value decision is simpler than dual-flag
Reversible by editing one config field
rationale.tradeoffs: Users who genuinely want strict zh-CN must opt in. Documented in the TASK-003 docs/configuration.md update.

manual_checks: Run scan on this dogfood project; verify the detected language is 'zh-CN-hybrid' (CJK README + English Skill technical terms)
Run scan on a fixture with EN-only README; verify the detected language is 'en'
Run scan on an empty repo; verify the detected language is 'match-existing' or null per current contract
success_metrics: scan-builders.test.ts CJK test passes with 'zh-CN-hybrid' assertion
integration: Coordination with TASK-003 schema: fabricLanguageSchema must accept 'zh-CN-hybrid' for scan-init.test.ts to pass end-to-end
